item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If not more of them. From what it sounds like, it's likely they ruled the roost before the Children of the Night overthrew them.

Heck, I wouldn't be surprised if the supposed trickster who was mentioned in SCP-1000 was one of them…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Humanity's predecessor's foundation… and we killed there people and now the monsters escaping.
heh

+1 you definitely deserve it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if I'm reading this right, they have the father of 073 and 076, as well as one of the strangers among the nameless trees? I got here from Taboo, and I absolutely love how all these different canons and skips intersect with each other. Gold star.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but I’m confused with the mention of “genetic matching organisms” in the containment procedures? They’re only mentioned there and not explained so I’m wondering what exactly they are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noticed this not to long ago. Titania is the name of the queen of the fairies in multiple sources of media. Now look at the last prisoner.

item.title: kaktus
item.content:encoded: king of scp-writer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Headcanon notes
– hide block

I'm a big believer in "there is no canon" and that readers should pick and choose which articles they think take place in the same universe.

That said, I personally consider this article to be easily compatible with SCP-4000. The Children of the Night created a Faerie Diaspora when they took over the world, but when the Fae are mentioned in 001:05, it's implied that they share the world with Humanity on a more-or-less equal level.

Why would Humanity choose to share their newly conquered society with the Fae? Probably because the Fae helped conquer it, teaming up with Humanity to compensate for whatever weakness the Bigfoots exploited. Then, wouldn't you know it, the Fae's new allies turn around and start a second Faerie Diaspora.

Why does "Fae" not trigger a nomenclative breach in this article? Because the Eshu phenomenon only affects the Fae from the Second Diaspora. Whatever weapon the Foundation used somehow broke/mutated the Fae's base relationship with name magic, which is why they appear so much more unstable in SCP-4000 than they do in traditional folklore. Any Fae who were sealed in SCP-2932 prior to the 1911 genocide wouldn't be affected by this.

Your mileage may vary, though~

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the links. I would not have remembered to look at the Factory for information about the faeries.

item.title: Re: oh
item.content:encoded: This seems quite plausible!

item.title: Interesting Meaning
item.content:encoded:
First off, this has always been one of my favorite SCPs on here, though Adam caught my attenton.

Namely because of what his name means.

"Adam" comes from a Hebrew "ha adamah" meaning "the ground" or "earth".

"El" refers to the Semitic term meaning God.

"Asem" is an alternate spelling to the Arabic name "Asim", meaning protector, guardian or defender.

So his name would mean "earth god protector". This does explain his capabilities and association to the land though I wonder what he was meant for.

item.title: Re: oh
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the necropost… but I am wondering one of these fairies was the so-called trickster god that helped humanity in order to screw over the Children of the Night…

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

So, the big link in this skip is the connection between the prison and SCP-1000. That being said, having a chance to talk to kaktus has offered a few other potential spots for links. Dr. Z. Johnson in the interview is a reference to Dr. Zachary Johnson from Last Meetings, while the mention of the Fae in the final cocoon in the log is a reference to Dr. Bright's 001. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Interviewer: Dr. Z. Johnson

To this:

Interviewer: Dr. Z. Johnson

Changing this:

Prisoner Name: Fae

To this:

Prisoner Name: Fae

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: Interesting paralells
item.content:encoded: Personally, I've always thought of 055 and 579 as opposites. The mysterious thing that's mysterious because nobody CAN know anything about it versus the mysterious thing that's mysterious because (implicitly) nobody SHOULD know anything about it. The similarity, in my mind, is that based on what little we know about Mal-Ga-Vor-Ta-Mor judging by the caretaker's description of him/it/whatever and what little we know about 579 judging by it's containment procedures, is that knowing anything about either is all but said to somehow be dangerous.

item.title: Re: Interesting paralells
item.content:encoded: 055 is considered to be a weak anti-meme. The caretaker fared better than many others.

item.title: Re: Interesting paralells
item.content:encoded:
That is who I meant, didn't remember the name off the top of my head. But if it were 55, how would the caretaker remember it? The ridiculous amounts of deliberate secrecy apparently involved in containing this guy personally made me think of 579, which is heavily implied to be some kind of deadly infohazard.

Furthermore… Wait, what were we talking about?

item.title: Re: Interesting paralells
item.content:encoded:
Some creatures brought into Titania's arms were lauded and their captors praised, but Mal-Va-Gar-Ta-Mor was brought in secret. Even I did not know of its incarceration until after it was done, but the Children assured me it was for the best that I was kept unaware. I remember when Mal-Va-Gar-Ta-Mor escaped, though, and the Children had not lied to me then. Sometimes it is best to not know.

If you're referring to that, I think of it more as a counterpart of SCP-055… or that Mal-Va-Gar-Ta-Mor is SCP-055!

item.title: Interesting paralells
item.content:encoded:
I had pondered the idea of a tale or something involving the 1000's Foundation-analouge.

Also, am I the only one for whom the prisoner we don't get any description of evoked 579?

item.title: Re: oh
item.content:encoded:
I know its a year late, but hell Ima answer it.
It probably refers to the fairies, a race of extremely powerful reality warpers mencioned in Brights 001 proposal. They where the rules of the world before 1000, who overthrew them. They are not fully dead though, and some of them managed to stay out (and where recovering until the Foundacion started to hunt them down as said in the proposal) and some are kept here.

item.title: Re: oh
item.content:encoded: I don't get it :/

item.title: oh
item.content:encoded:
Prisoner Name: Fae

…
Welp.

But yeah, this was pretty cool. Good worldbuilding. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I came back to it last night after simmering on it for a long time. Just didn't feel right that they wouldn't classify it as Thaumiel if they're not trying to crack it open and contain all the goodies inside of it. That's sort of the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yayy back to thaumiel

Loved this skip when I first read it, still love it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yes - it feels like the beginning of trying to make scp-1000 a GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, I was just about to comment that I think it should be Thaumiel. According to the locked box test this is a box = Thaumiel. It has cells for Cain and Abel, so on that basis alone it ought to be considered, I'd think (Thaumiel in regards to those two skips).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1, but there is a small error in the containment procedures

Information gathered form SCP-2932-A

Fix the word form

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs to be significantly higher rated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what the original 032 looked like. I had to have seen it at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Tempted to go with it for future material. Maybe.

item.title: Re: Upvoting with a vengeance, but. . .
item.content:encoded:
I think the reason this isn't Keter is because while they know the heart is dying, there is also very little risk of the things contained inside getting free until that happens. As a result, this falls under the general guideline of "not too difficult to contain, but keep an eye on it anyways" logic that Euclid tends to follow.

Also, forgot to +1 this before now. Whoops.

item.title: Upvoting with a vengeance, but. . .
item.content:encoded:
I love how this one ties-in with the material about "Bigfoot" (SCP-1000), as well as the hints concerning the origin/"father" of Cain and Able (073 and 076, of course). For those reasons and others, I am giving this a VERY enthusiastic upvote.

However, I do have ONE beef with this. As some others have been saying…shouldn't this be Keter? I mean seriously. It's quite clear that TITANIA'S HEART IS WEAKENING OVER TIME, this is heavily implied in the article. The "will of Titania" or whatever it is that sustains the prison already had to allow most of the less-powerful SCP's there escape just so that it could devote its waning, remaining power to the more serious threats still in containment. It seems like the Foundation has no idea whatsoever as to how to fix-up or maintain the facility, and neither does the weird organism acting as its warden. The only way to fix/maintain the facility that I can think of is to go actively seek-out populations of SCP-1000 and see if they have maintained records of this thing and how it works.

That being said, it's quite obvious that pretty much ALL the entities still imprisoned there are easily Keter-level threats. And the prison itself is slowly degrading and starting to fail. So shouldn't this by all means be considered a Keter-Level? For all we know, entities like Adam el Asem would even be capable of causing K-Class scenarios all on their own.

Edit: I was talking with Metaphysician about this, and she pointed out to me that the issue is that the things the "Warden" says about the various imprisoned entities may be exaggerations. Maybe they aren't really Keter-level. But that would imply great arrogance on the part of the Foundation, that they think they are somehow superior to the Children of the Night who we KNOW had much more advanced tech and knowledge. Basically, these are the things that the Children of the Night were UNABLE TO KILL. Doesn't that mean they MUST be Keter? Then there's the fact that one of them is clearly either Abel's dad, or of the same bloodline as Abel…and is apparently a reality-bender to boot. Can't we assume that the guy has GOT to be keter-level?

Bottom-line: Any chance we could get some of the Senior Staff to make an evaluation/re-evaluation of whether this one should be Euclid or Keter in the eyes of the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And i'm pretty sure Adam El Asem is their {and, by extension, our} daddy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's how I interpreted it as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Love it. The link to folklore is gone, but you reconstruct the whole thing into even more epic piece of work, good job. Referrence to SCP-1000 is also rare, you use it very well, and I welcome it. Excellent.

By the way is this…

…the one with desolation in his steps, and one for the other…

is it just me, or this is subtle referrence to 073 and 076?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh sweet. Upvoted. Though it's still tagged as Thaumiel.

I thought this would have been Keter, though I guess it depends on how escapable the prison is. I guess it's secure enough to be Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, I changed the object class. This probably makes more sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really, SCP 2000, SCP-1968 and SCP 179 (my favourite Thaumiels btw) are more about being highly classified even to most of the Foundation based on the nature of their abilities and roles. Usually thier ability to cause or prevent catastrophic events. This just seems like a prison. A prison with insanely powerful beings I'm assuming, but I dunno how that brings it up to Thaumiel. Or brings it across to Thaumiel, I should say.

I like this SCP itself, I just dont really like the classification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I don't have to miss the old 032 anymore! YAY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That wasn't clear? I describe a catastrophic event that happened to the creators, and then link specifically to the document detailing that event. The title of the link is the title of that section in 1000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Now see, I read that sentence as suggesting SCP-1000 was the catastrophic event, not the owners. Author, you might want to make that clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1000. They're nocturnal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not liking the sudden preponderance of Thaumiels. Those should be used even more sparingly than Keter.

Overall, I wasn't too interested in this. It kind of seems like a -001 without quite enough there. That said, I really like the descriptions of the inmates at the end, and I think the 1000 cross-reference works. Mostly, I'm just wondering, if we're the Children of the Sun, who the hell are the Children of the Night?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thaumiel isn't like, a level above Keter. If anything, it's off to the side. Thaumiel objects are objects used to contain other objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a newbie so I dunno how much my comment means to anyone but it seems real odd that this was classified as Thaumiel. Can someone explain how this would be important enough to be classified at that level? I got the impression that as long as Foundation Members started helping with the upkeep and guarding they wont have much chance to escape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see I see, for a while I was not sure if the language had that kind of verbal indication. If so then w/e. I know in my head I wouldn't pronounce children of the sun much more differently capitals or no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn you, djkaktus, damn you. This is the second time I had an idea for an SCP that got written up before I put hands to a keyboard. Here, take my upvote and leave me alone to brood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two reasons, 1) -A isn't a 1000, so there's no reason they'd use the same terminology, and 2) when those animals suddenly rise up and overthrow your entire society, you might be willing to change up your opinion of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's other languages that have indicators for the same thing we use capitalization to indicate without having the equivalent of capital letters- Ancient Egyptian used the Cartouche, and the Maya combined several symbols into a single compact tile, for instance.

They all represent that when you say a compound name like Children of the Sun out loud you say it differently, generally with a momentary pause or a slight vocal italicization, and compacted slightly. Thats why you pronounce it Ramses and not Ra Mus Es (Ra Bore Him) for instance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not familiar with Quechan but I'm vaguely under the impression that Quechan probably doesn't really have a verbal indication for capital letters? I have that as my concern when reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I'm getting this, it's only Thaumiel because it contains objects that haven't even been classified yet, right? Also, I thought humanity was looked down upon by SCP-1000 and treated like animals. Why the designation of the Children of the Sun?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very good. I like that this references -1000. I'm not sure if it gets referenced in other Scips, since I don't read very many often.
I also assume that some of the captives are also references to other Scips. But I wouldn't know what they might be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This truly is the new age of Thaumiel.

All hail the Thaumiel.

item.title: 2spooky4jungle
item.content:encoded:
Ok, so, in the vein of a number of other faux-rewrites, this is my re-imagining of Titania's Prison, an article that once occupied the 032 spot before being deleted by its author. Some references here and there, and I think it worked out just fine.

Thanks to everybody (TwistedGears) who looked this over in chat, and to the original authors of the things that are1 referenced throughout.

As always, your comments, critiques, and criticisms are always welcome!

Edit: Image is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Temple_%26_jungle._(3746632042).jpg

Footnotes
1. vaguely
